families.name: Hydrolytic bond cleavage
family_boundary_guardrails: Do not propagate labels across glycosidase, thiol protease, phosphorolysis, or nonhydrolytic metal-transfer chemistry without mechanism-text support.
Treat metal ligation as insufficient for hydrolase propagation unless water activation or hydrolytic bond cleavage is explicit.

families.name: ATP-dependent phosphoryl transfer
families.scope_note: Parent family for expert-reviewed ATP phosphoryl-transfer and ATP-coupled phospho-intermediate lanes surfaced by reaction/substrate mismatch review. This family is ontology boundary evidence only until a row has direct local mechanistic support and a countable seed fingerprint.
family_boundary_guardrails: Do not collapse ATP/Mg2+ phosphoryl-transfer or phospho-intermediate chemistry into metal hydrolase labels solely because ATP-bound Mg2+ or metal coordination is present.
Keep family assignments review-only unless local reaction-center evidence supports phosphoryl transfer and countable-import gates explicitly allow the label.

families.name: ePK-fold protein and ePK-like kinases
families.scope_note: Protein, lipid, and ePK-like antibiotic-resistance kinases using ATP gamma-phosphoryl transfer to hydroxyl acceptors. Includes ePK/RTK/MAPK-family and ePK-related lanes; excludes GHKL histidine kinases and ASKHA/Pfk sugar kinases.
family_boundary_guardrails: Treat ATP-bound Mg2+ as phosphoryl-transfer cofactor evidence, not as metal-hydrolase catalytic evidence.
Do not propagate from ePK-like names into countable labels without local acceptor-hydroxyl and ATP gamma-phosphate evidence.

families.name: ASKHA sugar and acetate kinases
families.scope_note: Actin-like/ASKHA kinases including hexokinase/glucokinase and acetate kinase lanes with ATP gamma-phosphoryl transfer to sugar hydroxyls or carboxylates.
family_boundary_guardrails: Keep hexokinase/glucokinase/acetate kinase review lanes out of hydrolase labels even when Mg2+ is local.
Do not merge ASKHA sugar kinases with PfkA, PfkB, or GHMP solely by ATP wording.

families.name: ATP-grasp ligases
families.scope_note: ATP-grasp ligases that form acyl-phosphate or related phosphorylated intermediates before ligation. This is ATP-coupled transfer/ligation chemistry, not hydrolytic bond cleavage.
family_boundary_guardrails: Do not count ATP-grasp rows as hydrolases because a phospho-intermediate later collapses.
Require ligase/phospho-intermediate context before mapping ATP-grasp boundary rows.

families.name: GHKL/Bergerat ATP-binding kinases
families.scope_note: Bergerat/GHKL ATP-binding kinases such as histidine kinases and pyruvate dehydrogenase kinase lanes. Kept separate from ePK and hydrolase chemistry.
family_boundary_guardrails: Do not propagate ePK rules into GHKL histidine-kinase or PDK lanes.
Local ATP/Mg2+ support must be interpreted as GHKL transfer evidence only with matching family context.

families.name: Deoxynucleoside kinases
families.scope_note: dNK-fold nucleoside kinases that transfer ATP gamma-phosphate to deoxynucleoside 5-prime hydroxyl acceptors. Kept separate from NDK phosphohistidine exchange.
family_boundary_guardrails: Do not merge dNK substrate hydroxyl phosphorylation with NDK ping-pong phosphohistidine chemistry.
Keep dNK lanes review-only until substrate-class and local phosphoryl-transfer evidence are explicit.

families.name: Nucleoside diphosphate kinases
families.scope_note: NDK-family nucleotide phosphate-transfer lanes using a phosphohistidine intermediate to exchange phosphate between nucleotides.
family_boundary_guardrails: Do not propagate NDK phosphohistidine chemistry into dNK substrate-hydroxyl phosphorylation.
Do not treat nucleotide phosphate transfer as metal hydrolase chemistry based on Mg2+ alone.

families.name: PfkA-fold phosphofructokinases
families.scope_note: PfkA-fold phosphofructokinase lanes transferring ATP gamma-phosphate to fructose-6-phosphate. Kept separate from PfkB ribokinase-like folds and ASKHA hexokinases.
family_boundary_guardrails: Do not infer PfkA from generic sugar kinase text without phosphofructokinase/PfkA evidence.
PfkA lanes remain hydrolase boundary controls until countable seed evidence is added.

families.name: PfkB/ribokinase-family kinases
families.scope_note: Ribokinase/PfkB-superfamily lanes including ribokinase and hydroxymethylpyrimidine kinase, transferring ATP gamma-phosphate to small-molecule hydroxyl acceptors.
family_boundary_guardrails: Do not collapse PfkB/ribokinase lanes into PfkA or ASKHA solely by sugar-kinase wording.
Keep PfkB family evidence review-only when local active-site support is incomplete.

families.name: GHMP-superfamily kinases
families.scope_note: GHMP-superfamily kinase lanes such as CDP-ME kinase, mevalonate-family kinase chemistry, and related ATP gamma-phosphoryl transfers. Kept separate from ASKHA, PfkA, and PfkB sugar kinases.
family_boundary_guardrails: Do not identify GHMP from ATP text alone; require GHMP-family or substrate-class evidence.
Do not count GHMP rows as hydrolases when ATP/Mg2+ is the local cofactor context.

families.name: Pyridoxal-phosphate chemistry
family_boundary_guardrails: Do not propagate between PLP decarboxylation, transamination, racemization, and beta-elimination subclasses without active-site lysine and substrate-class agreement.

families.name: Radical rearrangement chemistry
family_boundary_guardrails: Do not propagate radical labels between SAM/Fe-S and adenosylcobalamin systems solely by radical wording.
Require cofactor-family support or explicit mechanism text before treating radical families as compatible.

families.name: Flavin redox chemistry
family_boundary_guardrails: Separate oxygen insertion from hydride-transfer redox unless FAD/FMN context and reaction-center text support the subclass.
NAD-only hydride transfer is not enough to propagate a flavin label.

families.name: Heme peroxide and oxidase chemistry
family_boundary_guardrails: Do not propagate from generic heme or electron-transfer proteins into peroxide activation without distal activation residue or peroxide/oxygen chemistry support.

propagation_guardrails.rule: Family propagation requires shared mechanism family plus conserved catalytic role signatures; sequence cluster membership alone cannot promote labels above bronze.
Domain architecture can raise review priority but cannot promote across ontology families without reaction-center agreement.
Family annotations must be treated as weak evidence when mechanism text or cofactor evidence conflicts with the seed fingerprint.

When UniRef/CATH/InterPro are unavailable, use entry name, M-CSA mechanism text, cofactor ligands, and pocket geometry as auditable local proxies; keep propagated labels bronze until direct evidence promotes them.
